title: Dennis Campoverde-Lema
text:
Hello, my name is Dennis Campoverde-Lema. I am a dedicated student at Metropolitan State University in Saint Paul, Minnesota, majoring in Data Science and Industrial and Applied Mathematics. My academic journey is driven by a passion for uncovering insights through data and applying mathematical principles to solve real-world problems.
This website is a testament to my learning journey and a platform to showcase the various projects and personal research I have undertaken. I have built this site using Quarto and it is hosted on GitHub, highlighting my commitment to leveraging modern tools and technologies in my work.

What You’ll Find Here

Projects: A collection of my academic and personal projects, demonstrating my skills and growth in data science and applied mathematics.
Research: Insights and findings from my personal research endeavors, reflecting my curiosity and dedication to advancing knowledge in my field. Feel free to explore and learn more about my work. If you wish to connect or learn more, my GitHub, email, and resume are conveniently linked in the navbar.

Thank you for visiting my site!

title: Portfolio
text:
Welcome to my portfolio, where you will find a diverse collection of projects showcasing my skills and passion for data science and applied mathematics. This includes school projects demonstrating my ability to apply theoretical concepts to practical problems, research projects illustrating my capacity for rigorous investigation and innovative solution development, and personal projects driven by my curiosity and desire for continuous learning. Each project highlights my proficiency in data analysis, mathematical modeling, programming, and problem-solving. Explore my work and feel free to reach out with any questions or for further discussion.














Capstone





Capstone project on Social and Demographics factors that could affect drug overdose and alcohol impaired driving deaths within the counties of the United States for SURE 2024 at CMU





Jul 1, 2024












EDA: Hospital Ratings





Exploratory Data Analysis project on hospital ratings, mortality rates, procedure costs, and quality of care for SURE 2024 at CMU

title: Capstone
text:
Our project, “Understanding the Increase in Drug Overdose and Alcohol Driving Deaths,” examines how social and demographic factors impact drug overdoses and alcohol-impaired driving deaths across U.S. counties. We analyzed data from the University of Wisconsin Population Health Institute, focusing on predictors like unemployment, median income, and social associations. Using various models including Ridge, Lasso, Elastic Net, Linear Regression, and Random Forest, we aimed to uncover key predictors and provide actionable insights.
For detailed analysis and findings, click the “Report” button.

title: EDA: Hospital Ratings
text:
In our Exploratory Data Analysis (EDA) project using R, we analyzed Medicare and Medicaid data to gain insights into various aspects of hospital performance and healthcare quality. The dataset included information on different hospital types, such as government, public, and private-owned hospitals, along with mortality rates, Medicaid acceptance, costs, and quality of care metrics. One of our key objectives was to compare the mortality rates of different hospital types against the national average, which we visualized using stacked bar graphs. This analysis highlighted significant variations in mortality rates among different hospital types. Additionally, we identified hospitals that accept Medicaid and visualized their locations on maps, revealing the regional distribution and accessibility of these hospitals.
To further explore the relationship between cost and quality of care, we used box plots and violin plots to illustrate the distribution and density of cost data in relation to quality metrics. This analysis provided valuable insights into how cost impacts the quality of care, indicating potential areas for policy intervention. We also conducted a t-test to determine the statistical significance of the differences observed in costs and quality of care. Furthermore, we performed a clustering analysis to group hospitals based on various performance metrics, uncovering patterns and similarities among different hospital groups. For detailed analysis, visualizations, and code, please refer to our GitHub repository and slides by clicking the provided buttons.
